title: useReducer

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for useReducer; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to useReducer
sources.claim: useReducer centralizes related state transitions as pure actions from previous state to next state.

canonicalExplanation.summary: useReducer centralizes related state transitions as pure actions from previous state to next state.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A quiz reducer handles answer, next, reset, and finish while rejecting transitions after finish. Your goal is to refactor complex interdependent state into a tested reducer. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: State and events; html forms; Minimal and normalized state; javascript collections objects. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: useReducer centralizes related state transitions as pure actions from previous state to next state. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Reducers cannot perform effects, actions need clear events, and a reducer is unnecessary for one simple independent value. This draft therefore rejects: A reducer should fetch data directly when processing a load action.

includes: Represent related state transitions as explicit actions and a pure reducer.
Refactor complex interdependent state into a tested reducer.
excludes: Reducers cannot perform effects, actions need clear events, and a reducer is unnecessary for one simple independent value.

outcomes: Refactor complex interdependent state into a tested reducer.

examples.title: useReducer: concrete case
examples.situation: A quiz reducer handles answer, next, reset, and finish while rejecting transitions after finish.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A quiz reducer handles answer, next, reset, and finish while rejecting transitions after finish.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Reducers stay pure; perform effects outside and dispatch actions representing their results.
examples.result: The result follows because Reducers stay pure; perform effects outside and dispatch actions representing their results.

examples.title: useReducer: boundary case
examples.situation: Reducers cannot perform effects, actions need clear events, and a reducer is unnecessary for one simple independent value.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Reducers cannot perform effects, actions need clear events, and a reducer is unnecessary for one simple independent value.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: A reducer should fetch data directly when processing a load action.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Reducers stay pure; perform effects outside and dispatch actions representing their results.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A reducer should fetch data directly when processing a load action.
misconceptions.correction: Reducers stay pure; perform effects outside and dispatch actions representing their results.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete React: Component-Based Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: A reducer should fetch data directly when processing a load action.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model useReducer, where inputs and state produce a UI description.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for useReducer before using exact platform terms
limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.

recap.summary: You should now be able to refactor complex interdependent state into a tested reducer. Keep this boundary in view: Reducers cannot perform effects, actions need clear events, and a reducer is unnecessary for one simple independent value.

Why is this claim incorrect: A reducer should fetch data directly when processing a load action.
Apply checkpoint 'Reducers stay pure' to a new artifact.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Reducer and context boundary”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
